metadata.description: Terminal-native SOC skills for Claude Code, backed by Scanner MCP. Ports the n8n alert-triage, threat-hunting, and daily-reporting workflows to slash commands a SOC analyst can run from their laptop.

plugins.name: scanner-soc-skills
plugins.description: Eleven SOC and detection-engineering skills: triage-alert (lookup an alert by id and investigate), threat-hunt (sweep historical logs for fresh threat-intel IOCs), posture-report (daily Scanner posture report), investigate (free-form Scanner Q&A), lookup-ioc (IOC reputation across ThreatFox, OTX, Feodo Tracker), write-vrl (author and test VRL transformations), write-detection (author a new Scanner detection rule from natural language), tune-detection (investigate FPs and produce a tuning patch), migrate-detection (translate Splunk / Sigma / Chronicle / Panther / Sentinel / Elastic rules into Scanner YAML), write-correlation (author correlation rules joining constituent rules via custom tags), and recommend-detections (orchestrator that produces a prioritised list of detection-engineering next moves and routes the user to the other skills). Requires Scanner MCP configured in Claude Code; the skills also use SCANNER_API_URL / SCANNER_API_KEY for the Detection Rules REST API and `scanner-cli`, SCANNER_TENANT_ID for posture-report, OTX_API_KEY / ABUSECH_AUTH_KEY for threat intel, and SCANNER_DETECTIONS_DIR (comma-separated paths) for the detection-rule skills. write-vrl additionally needs a local `vector` binary; the detection-rule skills need `scanner-cli` on PATH.
